content: For server-side CSV generation in Next.js API routes: csv-stringify (v6.8.0, part of the csv monorepo, 940KB unpacked) is the mature stream-based choice. json2csv (v6.0.0-alpha.2) is only 51KB but still in alpha — avoid. For a small synchronous export like a 4-6 week calendar (< 50 rows), csv-stringify in synchronous mode is the correct pick: it's actively maintained, spec-compliant, zero new concepts beyond what the codebase already uses (transform streams), and the alpha status of json2csv makes it risky. Buffer CSV columns are: Text, Image URL, Tags, Posting Time (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm). Later does not have native CSV import as of 2025 (it's an open feature request on their community). Funūn should target Buffer column format as the primary export, with a note that Later users can import via Buffer or manual scheduling.
